title: Calculator Methodology
overview: This module provides an indicative catastrophe bond pricing and fiscal-cost estimate for policy analysis. It is intentionally simplified for scenario planning and should not be treated as execution pricing.

formulas.label: Estimated Spread (%)
formulas.expression: (Expected Loss % x Market Multiple) + Issuance Costs %

assumptions: Risk-free rate is represented by a simplified annual input (for example SOFR proxy).
Expected loss and market multiple are treated as static over the term.
Issuance costs are modeled as a percentage add-on to spread.
No time-varying coupon reset or collateral yield dynamics are modeled.
limitations: The calculator does not replace full catastrophe risk modeling, legal structuring, investor marketing, or transaction-level execution analytics.
Market multiples can shift materially across issuance windows and event cycles.
Basis risk, trigger design calibration, and payout timing effects are not fully captured.

sovereign_policy_mode.title: Sovereign Policy Mode
points: Expected loss connects directly to expected fiscal transfer from investors to government after qualifying events.
Ex-ante premium spending should be evaluated against post-disaster borrowing costs and budget shock volatility.
Catastrophe bonds can improve fiscal resilience by securing pre-arranged liquidity under transparent trigger rules.

disclaimer: For policy planning use only. Actual pricing requires live market soundings, catastrophe model outputs, legal documentation, and investor book-building.
